verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of mitochondrial F1-ATP synthase, canonically resident in the mitochondrial inner membrane facing the matrix. However, there is well-documented evidence that the F1-ATPase complex, including the beta subunit (also known as HEL-S-271 / ecto-F1-ATPase), translocates to the plasma membrane outer leaflet on certain cancer cells, hepatocytes, adipocytes, and endothelial cells, where it functions as a receptor/surface ATPase. This ecto-F1beta is a documented cancer-cell-state-induced surface target exploited by anti-tumor antibodies and natural ligands like angiostatin. This qualifies as contextual / cell_state_induced surface display.
